_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Pneumonia/ : Pneumonia_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Pneumonia_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
alos: 5 days (to be capped, new pre-auth to be raised after 5 days, if required, & should not be auto-approved)
clinical_indications: Sometimes bronchial breath sounds on examination

clinical_thresholds.note: For mortality risk assessment in primary care
For mortality risk assessment in primary care
For mortality risk assessment in primary care
For mortality risk assessment in primary care

claim.label: COVID testing (To be decided on case to case basis as per ICMR/ GoI guidelines and only in specific centres approved by GoI for doing such testing.)

ppd_preauth.q: Are the clinical sign and symptoms mentioned suggestive for Pneumonia?
Did chest X ray show any abnormality?

cpd_claim.q: Did Chest X ray show any abnormality?

common_queries: Clinical history or radiological findings not suggestive of Pneumonia.
X-ray/CT chest does not show any abnormality.
If COVID testing was advised, ICMR/GoI guidelines for testing were not strictly followed.
Missing mandatory clinical notes or X-ray/CT chest at pre-authorization.

clinical_key_pointers: Clinical picture of High fever (>39 degree)
Sometimes bronchial breath sounds on examination
Saturation O2 ≤92% and ≤90% for age ≤50 and >50 years
additional_information.post_op_care: Accepting orally, Afebrile and Hemodynamically stable for a period of at least 48 hours for discharge.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Oral antibiotics after checking for comorbidities (Diabetes, CVDs, CKD, CLD, Hepatic Pathology, Cancer, Alcohol Abuse, H/o antibiotics within last 3 months). If suspected atypical pathogens, other end organ disease, diabetes, malignancy, severe CAP, use of antibiotics in past 3 months. Suspected P. aeruginosa (diabetes, chronic lung disease like bronchiectasis, chronic steroid therapy) in ICU.
referral_criteria: Frank hemoptysis and/or Signs of respiratory failure
Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry ≤ 92% (patients ≤50 yrs) OR <90% (patients>50 yrs)
contraindications: Presence of severe upper airway inflammation & edema (for Non-invasive ventilation)
Severe haemodynamic instability - hypotension (for Non-invasive ventilation)
Multiple organ dysfunction or severe psychomotor agitation (for Non-invasive ventilation)
